title: FoxxyCode config.yaml
description: Configuration file for the FoxxyCode agent (default location: ~/.foxxycode/config.yaml). Reference: docs/config-reference.md. Any string value may reference environment variables with ${VAR_NAME}; ${FOXXYCODE_HOME} and ${CWD} are expanded by the loader.

providers.title: LLM providers
providers.description: API credentials and transport selection for upstream LLM vendors. When api_key is empty, the runtime reads the NAME_API_KEY environment variable (NAME is the provider name in uppercase with hyphens mapped to underscores).

name.description: Logical id used as the first segment of models[].model. ASCII letters, digits, hyphen, underscore; must start with a letter.

type.description: Wire protocol for this provider. Use "openai" for any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (OpenAI, DeepSeek, Groq, Ollama, llama.cpp, LM Studio). "neuraldeep" is OpenAI-compatible with a fixed endpoint.

api_base.description: Optional base URL override. For type "openai" include /v1 (e.g. http://localhost:11434/v1); for type "anthropic" an Anthropic-compatible gateway (default https://api.anthropic.com). Ignored for type "neuraldeep", which always uses https://api.neuraldeep.ru/v1.

api_key.description: Provider secret. A literal key, a "${ENV}" reference expanded at load time, or empty to read NAME_API_KEY at LLM call time. Resolution order: api_key -> api_key_command stdout -> NAME_API_KEY.

api_key_command.description: Optional credential-helper command. When api_key is empty it runs via the shell and the trimmed stdout is used as the key (like git/docker credential helpers). On failure resolution falls back to NAME_API_KEY.

proxy.description: Optional per-provider outbound proxy URL: http://, https://, socks5://, or socks5h:// (socks5h resolves hostnames via the proxy).

models.title: Logical models
models.description: Named model entries the agent and UI can select.

model.description: "provider_name/api_model_id": the first path segment must match a providers[].name; the remainder is sent to the LLM API (may itself contain slashes).

max_tokens.description: Upper bound on completion tokens per assistant message.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature (0 = deterministic; higher = more random).

max_context_tokens.description: Optional UI hint for the composer context bar; 0 derives it from provider metadata when available.

multimodal.description: Model accepts image/file inputs in addition to text; the UI shows a file attachment button for this model.

reasoning_levels.description: Override the reasoning levels offered for this model. Omit to auto-detect from the model id (gpt-5* -> minimal,low,medium,high; o-series and Claude thinking models -> low,medium,high). An explicit empty list hides the selector.

reasoning_default.description: Reasoning level pre-selected for new chats; must be one of the resolved levels, otherwise ignored.

agent.title: ReAct agent loop
agent.description: Defaults for the main agent loop (model id and safety caps).

model.description: Default models[].model id used until the client overrides the model per session. Required when models is non-empty.

max_turns.description: Hard cap on LLM calls per prompt turn.

max_tokens_per_turn.description: Max tokens across all LLM calls in one turn.

llm_retry_max.description: Retries after retryable LLM errors such as HTTP 429.

llm_retry_base_ms.description: Initial backoff between LLM retries, in milliseconds.

llm_min_interval_ms.description: Minimum gap between consecutive LLM calls in milliseconds (0 disables pacing; e.g. 12000 on strict free tiers).

prompts.title: System prompt templates
prompts.description: Override the built-in system prompt templates (Go text/template).

dir.description: Directory containing prompt template files. Empty uses embedded defaults. Supports ~ and ${CWD} (session cwd at render time).

agent_prompt.description: File name inside prompts.dir for agent mode.

plan_prompt.description: File name inside prompts.dir for plan mode.

docs_prompt.description: File name inside prompts.dir for docs mode.

instructions.title: Project instructions
instructions.description: Files read from the session working directory and appended to the system prompt (AGENTS.md convention).

files.description: Filenames relative to the session CWD.

skills.title: Skills
skills.description: Directories scanned for skills (SKILL.md and root .md/.mdc files).

dirs.description: Search paths; later entries win on name conflicts. Defaults (lowest to highest priority): ~/.agents/skills, ${FOXXYCODE_HOME}/skills, ${CWD}/.foxxycode/skills. ${FOXXYCODE_HOME} and ${CWD} expand at runtime.

rules.title: Project rules
rules.description: Discovery of rule files from .foxxycode/rules, .cursor/rules, .claude/rules, .codex/rules, and nested **/AGENTS.md under the session CWD. See docs/rules.md.

auto_discover.description: Scan the session CWD rule roots automatically.

systems.description: Restrict which rule systems are loaded: foxxycode, cursor, claude, codex, agents. Empty means all.

mcp_servers.title: MCP servers
mcp_servers.description: Model Context Protocol servers connected for every new session.

type.description: Transport. "stdio" (default when empty) runs a local command; "http" connects to a remote endpoint via url/headers.

name.description: Stable id referenced by the agent; must be unique in this list.

command.description: Executable for stdio transport (leave empty when using an http url).

args.description: Argv passed after command for stdio servers. ${CWD} expands to the session cwd.

env.description: Extra environment variables for the stdio child process.

value.description: Environment variable value. ${CWD} expands to the session cwd.

headers.description: Optional headers sent with MCP HTTP requests.

tools.title: Tools and permissions
tools.description: Filesystem and shell policy for built-in tools.

permission_mode.description: When the agent asks for user approval: "ask" prompts for commands and file writes; "accept_edits" auto-approves writes but prompts for commands; "bypass" never asks (trusted environments only).

command_allowlist.description: Commands that never require permission. Exact or prefix match (prefix + space + any args). "*" allows all commands.

ssh_connect_timeout.description: TCP dial timeout for SSH connections (ssh_run_command tool), in seconds.

logger.title: Logging
logger.description: Process log level, outputs, format, and file rotation.

level.description: Minimum severity written to the configured outputs ("warning" is accepted as an alias of "warn").

outputs.description: Where log records go; any combination of stdout, stderr, file. Empty means stderr only.

file.description: Path for the file sink; required when outputs includes "file". Supports ${FOXXYCODE_HOME}.

rotation.description: Size-based rotation for the file sink.

max_size_mb.description: Rotate after the file reaches this size in MB; 0 disables size-based rotation.

max_files.description: Rotated backups to keep when max_size_mb > 0.

sessions.title: Session storage
sessions.description: Where persisted session bundles are stored.

dir.description: Sessions root directory. Empty resolves to ${FOXXYCODE_HOME}/sessions. Supports ${FOXXYCODE_HOME} and ~.

memory.title: Long-term memory copilot
memory.description: Optional memory copilot (implementation in external/memory; enable at runtime with memory.enabled).

model.description: Exact models[].model id used only for recall/persist LLM calls; empty falls back to agent.model or the session override.

dir.description: Long-term memory root. Empty resolves to ${FOXXYCODE_HOME}/memory. Supports ${FOXXYCODE_HOME} and ~.

recall_max_turns.description: Bounds recall-side LLM rounds in the memory loop.

persist_max_turns.description: Bounds persist-side LLM rounds in the memory loop.

copilot_max_tokens.description: Completion token cap for memory copilot LLM calls.

httpserver.title: HTTP gateway
httpserver.description: OpenAI-compatible HTTP API defaults (used only by binaries built with -tags http; the embedded SPA needs -tags http,ui). See docs/http-api.md.

host.description: Default bind address when foxxycode http does not pass -H/--host. Empty falls back to 0.0.0.0.

port.description: Default listen port when foxxycode http does not pass -P/--port. 0 falls back to 12345.

scheduler.title: Cron scheduler
scheduler.description: Cron-driven scheduled jobs (used only by binaries built with -tags scheduler). Jobs are flat *.md files with YAML frontmatter under scheduler.dir; five-field crontab in UTC.

enabled.description: Run the scheduler daemon and expose the foxxycode_scheduler_* tools. Can also be forced per process with foxxycode acp|http -scheduler-enabled.

dir.description: Directory with *.md job definitions. Empty resolves to ${FOXXYCODE_HOME}/scheduler.

max_queue.description: Concurrent scheduled runs; when saturated, extra firings are skipped until a slot frees.

timeout.description: Wall-clock limit for one scheduled agent run, as a Go duration (e.g. "30m", "1h30m").

retain_sessions.description: Completed scheduler-run session directories kept per job_id under sessions.dir; older runs are pruned.

gateways.title: Messenger gateways
gateways.description: Messenger bot adapters (used only by binaries built with -tags gateway or -tags gateway.telegram; run with foxxycode gateway). See docs/gateway.md.

enabled.description: Activate the Telegram adapter when foxxycode gateway starts.

token.description: Bot token from @BotFather. Leave empty to read the TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN environment variable (e.g. via ~/.foxxycode/.env).

proxy.description: Optional outbound proxy for Telegram API requests: http, https, socks5, or socks5h URL.

rich_messages.description: Use Bot API 10.1 Rich Messages (native Markdown, streamed thinking placeholder, collapsible tool list). Falls back to legacy formatting when unsupported.

admins.description: Telegram user IDs with elevated rights; admins always pass access checks.

default_access.description: Fallback access level for chats without an override: "all", "admins", or "group:<name>".

default_isolation.description: Fallback session isolation for group chats: "individual" (session per user), "shared" (one session for all), "admin" (admins only, shared session).

user_groups.description: Named sets of user IDs referenced from access fields as group:<name>.

user_ids.description: Telegram numeric user IDs in this group.

chats.description: Per-chat overrides; chat_id is negative for groups and supergroups.

ui.title: UI
ui.description: Embedded SPA preferences for desktop and HTTP UI.

locale.description: UI locale for the embedded SPA. Empty means auto-detect from the system or browser locale.
